mechanism_narrative: Current level of consumer price inflation erodes real purchasing power and forces the central bank to tighten, leading to negative equity returns over the next month. High CPIAUCSL directly depresses future cash flow valuations.
failure_condition: Spearman rank correlation between CPIAUCSL and 20-day forward SPX returns is non-negative over any rolling 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate rises above 5.0 percent, it signals incipient recession risk as firms tighten hiring and consumer spending weakens, causing equity prices to fall over the following 20 trading days.
failure_condition: In periods when UNRATE exceeds 5%, the proportion of positive 20-day SPX returns is above 50% (indicating a false negative signal).

mechanism_narrative: The joint elevation of industrial production and payroll employment signals an overheating economy that provokes monetary tightening, causing negative S&P 500 returns over the subsequent quarter.
failure_condition: When both INDPRO and PAYEMS are in their top historical tertile, the average 60-day SPX return is not negative and statistically different from zero at 5% significance.

mechanism_narrative: In a monetary tightening regime (FEDFUNDS elevated), rising housing starts add to demand pressure, forcing further tightening and depressing forward equity returns.
failure_condition: In months when FEDFUNDS exceeds 0.5%, the rank IC between HOUST and 20-day SPX returns is not negative.

mechanism_narrative: When monthly retail sales exceed 460 billion dollars, it reflects strong consumer demand that drives corporate earnings higher, leading to positive S&P 500 returns over the next week.
failure_condition: Average 5-day SPX return when RSAFS > 460000 is not positive or fails a one-sided t-test with p < 0.10.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production above an index level of 105 signals robust manufacturing output and economic expansion, which translates into positive short-term equity returns.
failure_condition: 5-day SPX returns when INDPRO > 105 are not significantly positive (t-test p > 0.10) compared to all other periods.

mechanism_narrative: The combination of low unemployment and high inflation creates a stagflation threat where the central bank faces a policy dilemma, and equity markets de-rate due to margin pressure and uncertainty, producing negative returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: When UNRATE is below 5% and CPIAUCSL is above 240 (both historical medians), the 60-day SPX return is not negative on average.

mechanism_narrative: During periods of labor market weakness (unemployment above 5.5 percent), an increase in housing starts signals the beginning of a recovery in residential investment, which boosts economic confidence and corporate earnings, leading to positive equity returns over 20 days.
failure_condition: In months when UNRATE > 5.5%, the correlation between HOUST level and 20-day SPX returns is non-positive, or the mean return conditional on high HOUST is not higher than unconditional mean.
